message.content:
Design a detailed implementation plan for a new `show-session` subcommand in the Rust CLI at /home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1. Return the plan as your final text (do not write files).

## Purpose
Display a Claude Code transcript `.jsonl` file (session logs; this project keeps them in its `.claude/` bot repo, e.g. `.claude/4f5d498c-da5b-40ca-820e-a89676a58d79.jsonl`) as a readable conversation. Long-term goal (later cycles, not now): link prompts to the commits they produced, follow cross-file references (subagent sidechain files, compaction chains), an index view, session discovery.

## Agreed decisions (binding)
- Two-layer parse: serde_json used ONLY as a JSON-text → `serde_json::Value` parser (new dependency, Value API only, NO serde derive anywhere). Hand-written extraction from Value into our own plain Rust structs (the "typed layer") that we evolve over time. Unknown fields ride along in the Value; missing fields degrade gracefully.
- v1 input: positional FILE path only.
- v1 output: conversation view — user/assistant text in full, tool calls as one-liners (tool name + gist of input), thinking/meta/attachments/bookkeeping hidden by default, flags to reveal more.
- Command name: `show-session`.

## Codebase architecture (from exploration — follow these patterns)
- Single crate, clap 4 derive, edition 2024, version 0.69.2. Existing deps: chrono (default-features=false, features=["now"] — no parse/format features), clap, clap_complete, jj-lib, log, pollster. NO serde today.
- Subcommand pattern (see src/chid.rs, src/validate_todo.rs as models): `XxxArgs` (#[derive(clap::Args)]) → `XxxParams` (clap-free, via From/TryFrom<&Args>, error String) → `fn xxx(ctx: &Context, params: &XxxParams) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>>`. Each Args implements `SubcommandRunner` (src/subcommand.rs) with to_params/run; registered as a variant in `enum Commands` in src/main.rs plus one `Commands::Xxx(args) => args.dispatch(&ctx)` line.
- Errors: no anyhow; `Box<dyn Error>` + `format!(...).into()` / map_err with format!.
- Output: through `log` crate — `info!` → stdout for user output, debug!/trace! → stderr under -v. ANSI bold helpers in src/common.rs (`bold`, `bold_first_line`, `indent_body`). No table/TUI crates; manual format strings.
- Project lints: `unwrap_used = "warn"`, `expect_used = "warn"`; any unwrap/expect/unwrap_or* needs `// OK: reason` comment (and #[allow] for the panicking ones). Prefer shapes that avoid them.
- Doc comments mandatory: `//!` module header on every file, `///` on every fn/struct/field, intro + bullets style.
- Tests: in-module `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` or sibling file via `#[path]` (e.g. src/common/tests.rs); CLI integration tests in tests/ using tests/common/mod.rs `CliFixture` (tempdir, HOME override, fx.cmd() runs the binary, run_ok/run_err). validate_todo/fix_todo tests are the file-reading precedent.
- src/validate_todo.rs is the closest analog: `#[arg(value_name="FILE", ...)] file: PathBuf`, reads with fs::read_to_string mapped to a friendly error.

## Real data survey (from the project's .claude/*.jsonl, ~65k lines total)
Top-level `type` histogram: assistant 29120, user 19087, file-history-snapshot 4776, permission-mode 3064, last-prompt 3016, system 2831, progress 2363, ai-title 2249, attachment 2050, mode 699, queue-operation 131, file-history-delta 54, custom-title 2, agent-name 2.
- assistant entries: one JSONL line PER CONTENT BLOCK — message.content is an array with a single-ish block: tool_use, thinking, or text. Top-level keys seen: cwd, entrypoint, gitBranch, isSidechain, message, parentUuid, requestId, sessionId, session_id, timestamp, type, userType, uuid, version.
- user entries: either message.content as plain string (typed prompt; has promptSource, origin, permissionMode, promptId) or array of tool_result blocks (has toolUseResult, sourceToolAssistantUUID).
- isMeta flag marks injected/meta user lines. isSidechain marks subagent lines.
- Files can have a truncated final line (live session being appended) — must tolerate.
- Message content blocks (from Claude Code format generally): text {text}, thinking {thinking}, tool_use {id,name,input}, tool_result {tool_use_id, content: string|array of {type:text,...}, is_error}.

## What the plan must cover
1. Module layout: proposed new files (e.g. a transcript parsing/model module and src/show_session.rs command module — or a different split if better), what goes where, rough struct/fn signatures for the typed layer (entry enum? common fields struct? content block enum?) and the extraction API from Value (helper fns like get_str(path)). Design the typed layer so later cycles can add cross-file reference following (agent files, leafUuid) without restructuring.
2. Renderer design: conversation view formatting (role headers, timestamps, wrapping?, ANSI bold, tool_use one-liners, tool_result default-hidden), proposed flags for v1 (keep small: e.g. --meta/--thinking/--tools verbosity flags, maybe --raw for the Value dump of an entry range?) — recommend a minimal but coherent v1 flag set.
3. Unparseable/unknown handling: unknown types silently skipped in conversation view but countable; malformed lines → warning to stderr + continue; truncated last line tolerated.
4. Cargo.toml change: serde_json dependency line (consider default features; serde_json pulls serde as a lib dep — that's accepted).
5. Test plan: unit tests for extraction (inline JSON fixtures), renderer tests, CLI integration test with a small fixture .jsonl (which fixture entries to include).
6. Step ladder: split the work into 2-4 incremental commits suitable for this project's cycle protocol (each commit compiles, passes fmt/clippy/test). Suggest what lands in each: e.g. -1 parser+typed layer with tests, -2 renderer+command wiring+integration tests. (A -0 Preparation commit for TODO/chores/version bookkeeping exists separately; don't design it.)

Keep the plan concrete: file paths, type names, function signatures, flag names. Flag any risks or open questions at the end.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- artifact-capabilities: Runtime capabilities a published Artifact can declare — calling the user's claude.ai connectors (MCP) from the page, and future abilities. Load this BEFORE passing `capabilities` to the Artifact tool or writing any `window.claude.mcp` code.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- claude-in-chrome: Automates your Chrome browser to interact with web pages - clicking elements, filling forms, capturing screenshots, reading console logs, and navigating sites. Opens pages in new tabs within your existing Chrome session. Requires site-level permissions before executing (configured in the extension). - When the user wants to interact with web pages, automate browser tasks, capture screenshots, read console logs, or perform any browser-based actions. Always invoke BEFORE attempting to use any mcp__claude-in-chrome__* tools.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	//! `SubcommandRunner` trait
2	//!
3	//! The purpose is to simplify invoking a subcommand so `main`
4	//! can invoke any subcommand with one line:
5	//!
6	//! `Commands::Chid(args) => args.dispatch(&ctx),`
7	//!
8	//! Instead of the ~11 lines that the default `dispatch` below
9	//! now encapsulates.
10	//!
11	//! Implementors provide:
12	//!
13	//! - [`SubcommandRunner::to_params`] — build the clap-free
14	//!   `Params` from the clap `Args` (covers both `From` /
15	//!   `TryFrom` shapes uniformly via `Result<_, String>`).
16	//! - [`SubcommandRunner::run`] — the subcommand body.
17	//!
18	//! The default [`SubcommandRunner::dispatch`] builds `Params`
19	//! via `to_params`, runs via `run`, and maps the result to
20	//! `ExitCode`. Errors at any stage log via `error!` and return
21	//! `ExitCode::FAILURE`. `Context` is loaded once in `main` and
22	//! passed in by reference so a single `Context` is shared across
23	//! the (one) match arm that actually runs.
24	//!
25	//! ## See also
26	//!
27	//! - The wider CLI-args / subcommand-layer split this trait
28	//!   formalizes: [`ARCHITECTURE.md > args → Context + Params`][arch].
29	//! - Cycle design, ladder, evaluation gate:
30	//!   [`chores-10.md > 0.50.0-0`][open].
31	//! - Worked example, trait-shape decisions, naming:
32	//!   [`chores-10.md > 0.50.0-1`][port].
33	//! - First implementor: [`crate::chid`].
34	//!
35	//! [arch]: https://github.com/winksaville/vc-x1/blob/main/ARCHITECTURE.md#args--context--params
36	//! [open]: https://github.com/winksaville/vc-x1/blob/main/notes/chores/chores-10.md#chore-open-subcommand-trait-sweep-0500-0
37	//! [port]: https://github.com/winksaville/vc-x1/blob/main/notes/chores/chores-10.md#refactor-subcommandrunner-trait--chid-0500-1
38	
39	use std::process::ExitCode;
40	
41	use log::error;
42	
43	use crate::context::Context;
44	
45	/// Trait implemented by every subcommand's clap `Args` type so
46	/// `main.rs` can dispatch via a single `args.dispatch(&ctx)`
47	/// call.
48	pub trait SubcommandRunner {
49	    /// The clap-free `Params` struct the subcommand body
50	    /// consumes.
51	    type Params;
52	
53	    /// Build `Params` from `Args`. `Result<_, String>` absorbs
54	    /// both `From` (return `Ok(...)`) and `TryFrom` (forward
55	    /// the error) shapes uniformly.
56	    fn to_params(&self) -> Result<Self::Params, String>;
57	
58	    /// Run the subcommand body.
59	    fn run(ctx: &Context, params: &Self::Params) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>>;
60	
61	    /// Default dispatch: build `Params` via `to_params`, bracket
62	    /// the run with `crate::bm_track` enter/exit, execute via
63	    /// `run`, and map the result to `ExitCode`. Errors at any
64	    /// stage log via `error!` and return `ExitCode::FAILURE`.
65	    /// `bm_track` itself emits at `debug!`, so default runs stay
66	    /// quiet — no per-call gate is needed here.
67	    fn dispatch(&self, ctx: &Context) -> ExitCode {
68	        let params = match self.to_params() {
69	            Ok(p) => p,
70	            Err(e) => {
71	                error!("{e}");
72	                return ExitCode::FAILURE;
73	            }
74	        };
75	
76	        // Command name is the first positional after the binary;
77	        // clap has already validated it by the time we reach
78	        // dispatch (top-level parse errors exit earlier).
79	        let command_name = std::env::args().nth(1).unwrap_or_else(|| "?".to_string()); // OK: default when somehow invoked without a subcommand
80	
81	        crate::bm_track("enter", &command_name);
82	        let exit_code = match Self::run(ctx, &params) {
83	            Ok(()) => ExitCode::SUCCESS,
84	            Err(e) => {
85	                error!("{e}");
86	                ExitCode::FAILURE
87	            }
88	        };
89	        crate::bm_track("exit ", &command_name);
90	        exit_code
91	    }
92	}

42	/// Banner string emitted as the first line of normal command runs
43	/// and shown at the top of subcommand `--help` output. Built from
44	/// `Cargo.toml`'s name + version at compile time so it stays in
45	/// sync with the bumped version.
46	const BANNER: &str = concat!(env!("CARGO_PKG_NAME"), " ", env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION"));
47	
48	/// Top-level about line — name, version, and the project tagline
49	/// on a single line. Used as the top-level `about` so `vc-x1 -h`
50	/// reads as one banner-plus-tagline header instead of two stacked
51	/// lines.
52	const TOP_ABOUT: &str = concat!(
53	    env!("CARGO_PKG_NAME"),
54	    " ",
55	    env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION"),
56	    " - jj workspace tooling"
57	);
58	
59	/// Build the clap command tree with `BANNER` set as `before_help`
60	/// on every subcommand (transitively). Top-level skips `before_help`
61	/// because its own `about` already carries the name+version+tagline.
62	/// Walks via `mut_subcommand` so individual subcommand
63	/// `#[command(long_about = ...)]` blocks don't have to repeat the
64	/// banner text.
65	fn cli_with_banner() -> clap::Command {
66	    fn add_to_subs(mut cmd: clap::Command) -> clap::Command {
67	        let names: Vec<String> = cmd
68	            .get_subcommands()
69	            .map(|c| c.get_name().to_string())
70	            .collect();
71	        for name in names {
72	            cmd = cmd.mut_subcommand(name, add_with_banner);
73	        }
74	        cmd
75	    }
76	    fn add_with_banner(mut cmd: clap::Command) -> clap::Command {
77	        cmd = cmd.before_help(BANNER);
78	        let names: Vec<String> = cmd
79	            .get_subcommands()
80	            .map(|c| c.get_name().to_string())
81	            .collect();
82	        for name in names {
83	            cmd = cmd.mut_subcommand(name, add_with_banner);
84	        }
85	        cmd
86	    }
87	    add_to_subs(Cli::command())
88	}

216	        pre-sync op id is persisted to `.vc-x1/sync-state.toml`; undo\n\
217	        explicitly with `vc-x1 revert` (state is cleared on success).\n\n\

220	          - action needed:  per-repo fetch + state + actions\n  \
221	          - --quiet:        no output; exit code signals success", sync::UP_TO_DATE_MSG))]

256	        squash-push the bot repo's `main` — one resumable command.\n\n\
257	        Collapses the manual commit-push-publish ceremony into a\n\
258	        single subcommand with two interactive approval gates and a\n\
259	        state machine with persistent progress so interruptions can\n\
260	        resume without re-doing completed stages.\n\n\
261	        Stages: preflight (tracking / bot-published / sync checks —\n\
262	        no build steps; run project checks yourself before pushing)\n\
263	        → review (approve diff)\n\

278	/// Permanent sanity check for the `main`-bookmark tracking state
279	/// in both repos of the dual-repo workspace. Emits one line on
280	/// entry and one on exit of every command. If entry and exit
281	/// differ, the executing command is the culprit; if entry differs
282	/// from the previous command's exit, something *between*
283	/// invocations broke it. Originally added in 0.37.2 as a temporary
284	/// diagnostic; promoted to permanent in 0.37.4 after the user
285	/// reported "happens more than once".
286	///
287	/// Emits at `log::debug!` (since 0.52.0-1) — default runs stay
288	/// quiet, and the signal remains available under `-v` when
289	/// investigating.
290	///
291	/// Walks up from cwd to locate the workspace root (the directory
292	/// whose `.vc-config.toml` has `path = "/"`), then probes `<root>`
293	/// and `<root>/.claude`. Same labeling whether the user runs from
294	/// the work root, from `.claude`, or from any subdir.
295	pub fn bm_track(phase: &str, command_name: &str) {
296	    let header = format!("bm-track {phase} vc-x1 {command_name}");
297	    let root = match common::find_workspace_root() {
298	        Some(r) => r,
299	        None => {
300	            log::debug!("{header}: no-workspace");
301	            return;
302	        }
303	    };
304	    let repos: [(std::path::PathBuf, &str); 2] =
305	        [(root.clone(), "work"), (root.join(".claude"), ".claude")];
306	    let mut parts: Vec<String> = Vec::new();
307	    for (repo, label) in repos {
308	        if !repo.join(".jj").exists() {
309	            parts.push(format!("{label}(main)=no-jj"));
310	            continue;
311	        }
312	        match bm_track_one(&repo, "main", "origin") {
313	            Ok(true) => parts.push(format!("{label}(main)=tracked")),
314	            Ok(false) => parts.push(format!("{label}(main)=NOT_TRACKED")),
315	            Err(e) => parts.push(format!(
316	                "{label}(main)=err({})",
317	                e.lines().next().unwrap_or("")
318	            )),
319	        }
320	    }
321	    log::debug!("{header}: {}", parts.join(", "));
322	}
323	
324	/// Query jj for whether `bookmark` in `repo` is tracking `remote`.
325	/// Returns `Ok(true)` if the tracked-list entry for `bookmark` shows
326	/// an `@<remote>` line (in any form — `@origin:` when synced, or
327	/// `@origin (ahead by N commits):` / similar when divergent — both
328	/// still count as tracking). `Ok(false)` when no such line exists
329	/// (bookmark isn't tracking this remote or doesn't exist). `Err` on
330	/// subprocess failure.
331	fn bm_track_one(repo: &Path, bookmark: &str, remote: &str) -> Result<bool, String> {
332	    let repo_str = repo.to_string_lossy();
333	    let output = std::process::Command::new("jj")
334	        .args(["bookmark", "list", "--tracked", bookmark, "-R", &repo_str])
335	        .output()
336	        .map_err(|e| format!("spawn: {e}"))?;
337	    if !output.status.success() {
338	        return Err(String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr).trim().to_string());
339	    }
340	    let stdout = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout);
341	    // Accept both the synced form (`@origin:`) and the decorated
342	    // divergent form (`@origin (ahead by N commits):`), which still
343	    // represents a tracking relationship.
344	    let colon = format!("@{remote}:");
345	    let paren = format!("@{remote} ");
346	    Ok(stdout.lines().any(|l| {
347	        let t = l.trim_start();
348	        t.starts_with(&colon) || t.starts_with(&paren)
349	    }))
350	}
351	
352	fn main() -> ExitCode {
353	    CompleteEnv::with_factory(cli_with_banner).complete();
354	    let matches = cli_with_banner().get_matches();
355	    let cli = match Cli::from_arg_matches(&matches) {
356	        Ok(cli) => cli,
357	        Err(e) => {
358	            e.exit();
359	        }
360	    };
361	
362	    let log_path = cli.log.as_ref().map(|p| p.to_string_lossy().to_string());
363	    logging::CliLogger::init(cli.verbose, log_path.as_deref());
364	
365	    // `-V` / `--version`: emit the `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner as the
366	    // first line and continue. With no subcommand, the banner is
367	    // the whole invocation. Uniform `BANNER` (not the
368	    // `vc-x1-<sub>` form clap's `propagate_version` would print)
369	    // — the version is the binary's regardless of which
370	    // subcommand it routes to.
371	    if cli.version {
372	        log::info!("{BANNER}");
373	    }
374	
375	    let Some(cmd) = cli.command else {
376	        // No subcommand. If `-V` was set the banner has already
377	        // printed, so exit success; otherwise mirror clap's "a
378	        // subcommand is required" error by printing usage and
379	        // exiting non-zero.
380	        if cli.version {
381	            return ExitCode::SUCCESS;
382	        }
383	        let mut cmd = cli_with_banner();
384	        let _ = cmd.print_help();
385	        return ExitCode::FAILURE;
386	    };
387	
388	    let ctx = match context::Context::load() {
389	        Ok(c) => c,
390	        Err(e) => {
391	            error!("{e}");
392	            return ExitCode::FAILURE;
393	        }
394	    };
395	
396	    match cmd {
397	        Commands::Chid(args) => args.dispatch(&ctx),
398	        Commands::Desc(args) => args.dispatch(&ctx),
399	        Commands::List(args) => args.dispatch(&ctx),
400	        Commands::Show(args) => args.dispatch(&ctx),
401	        Commands::ValidateBot(args) => args.dispatch(&ctx),
402	        Commands::ValidateDesc(args) => args.dispatch(&ctx),
403	        Commands::FixDesc(args) => args.dispatch(&ctx),
404	        Commands::ValidateTodo(args) => args.dispatch(&ctx),
405	        Commands::FixTodo(args) => args.dispatch(&ctx),
406	        Commands::Clone(args) => args.dispatch(&ctx),
407	        Commands::Init(args) => args.dispatch(&ctx),
408	        Commands::Symlink(args) => args.dispatch(&ctx),
409	        Commands::Sync(args) => args.dispatch(&ctx),
410	        Commands::Revert(args) => args.dispatch(&ctx),
411	        Commands::SquashPush(args) => args.dispatch(&ctx),
412	        Commands::Push(args) => args.dispatch(&ctx),
413	    }
414	}
